summary: PR 1576 reduced dtk auto-generated splits by explicitly adding previously implicit Runtime and sysdolphin sections to config/GALE01/splits.txt and matching small Runtime objects that were easy to recover. It added split entries for Runtime/global_destructor_chain.c, Runtime/__init_cpp_exceptions.c, and a missing hsd_40FF.c .sdata2 range, then adjusted Runtime code to emit ctor/dtor section references using a new SECTION_DTORS macro. The PR body says the only remaining auto-split after this was MetroTRK exception.s, which was acceptable because it is assembly.

key_files.role: Added explicit split ranges for Runtime/global_destructor_chain.c, Runtime/__init_cpp_exceptions.c, and sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_40FF.c .sdata2 to eliminate auto-generated split objects.
Renamed the extern from the temporary _eti_init_info_ hack to _eti_init_info and added ctor/dtor section references for exception init/fini and global destructor chain functions.
Introduced SECTION_DTORS, mirroring section macro style for Metrowerks builds while remaining empty for m2ctx/non-MWERKS contexts.
Added a SECTION_DTORS reference to __destroy_global_chain so the destructor chain object can be represented explicitly.

what_changed: Added an explicit Runtime/global_destructor_chain.c split with .text 0x80322714-0x8032275C, .dtors 0x803B7268-0x803B726C, and .sbss 0x804D7058-0x804D705C.
Added an explicit Runtime/__init_cpp_exceptions.c split with .text 0x80322F20-0x80322F9C, .ctors 0x803B7240-0x803B7244, .dtors 0x803B7260-0x803B7268, and .sdata 0x804D5B40-0x804D5B44.
Replaced the prior _eti_init_info_ workaround in __init_cpp_exceptions.c with the intended _eti_init_info symbol and passed it to __register_fragment.

Added SECTION_CTORS/SECTION_DTORS pointer references for __init_cpp_exceptions, __destroy_global_chain, and __fini_cpp_exceptions so ctor/dtor entries are emitted as normal source-backed data rather than auto splits.
Added SECTION_DTORS to platform.h with __declspec(section ".dtors") under Metrowerks except when M2CTX is defined.
smart_moves: Turned auto-split artifacts into explicit split entries and source-backed section references, making the object layout more intentional and searchable.
Kept section macros compiler-aware: SECTION_DTORS expands to a Metrowerks section declaration only for real MWERKS builds and stays empty for tooling/non-MWERKS contexts.
Removed a documented temporary linker naming workaround for _eti_init_info_ once the split/source arrangement allowed the intended _eti_init_info name.
Handled both code and data/section boundaries: the PR did not only add functions, it also accounted for .ctors, .dtors, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2 ranges that dtk had been generating implicitly.
decomp_lessons: When dtk auto-generates split objects, check whether the content is small source-recoverable Runtime data or a missing section range before leaving it as an auto split.
Ctor and dtor table entries can often be modeled as section-placed function pointer references in C, reducing generated data fragments.
For Runtime startup/teardown code, matching may require representing special sections such as .ctors and .dtors in both splits.txt and source annotations.
If a symbol name is being worked around because the linker cannot generate it, revisit the workaround after adding the correct split sections; the intended symbol name may become usable.
Missing SDA/SDA2 ranges can appear as auto-splits; adding the correct .sdata2 range in splits.txt can eliminate a generated object without needing new C code.
naming_conventions: Use the real Runtime exception table symbol name _eti_init_info instead of a temporary suffixed workaround such as _eti_init_info_ when the linker/split setup supports it.
Section reference symbols in this diff used descriptive names ending in _reference, e.g. __init_cpp_exceptions_reference, __destroy_global_chain_reference, and __fini_cpp_exceptions_reference.
Section macros follow the existing uppercase SECTION_* style, with SECTION_DTORS added alongside existing section annotation macros.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use __declspec(section ".dtors") through a SECTION_DTORS wrapper to place destructor references in the exact .dtors section for MWERKS builds.
Use SECTION_CTORS and SECTION_DTORS function pointer constants to force ctor/dtor table entries from C source.
For MWERKS_GEKKO, __init_cpp_exceptions keeps the inline assembly GetR2 helper and registers the fragment with __register_fragment(_eti_init_info, GetR2()).
The PR body explicitly notes that MetroTRK exception.s remained as the only auto-split and was likely acceptable because it is assembly.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explains the motivation: unspecified code/data in splits.txt is auto-generated by dtk, some generated objects were easy to match, and another was a missing sda2 segment.
